title: Last Judgement of Hunefer
topic_path: New Kingdom and Third Intermediate Period

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the New Kingdom, Books of the Dead were created for $\_\_\_\_\_\_$ .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Although similar sacred texts were reserved for kings in the Old Kingdom, Books of the Dead were made for scribes, priests, and other high ranking individuals in the New Kingdom.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How many times does Hunefer appear in this image from the Book of the Dead?** 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da13322fae297f9df8309fdb72bfd052.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Wearing a white robe, Hunefer appears 3 times:  in the upper left corner before a line of divinities; on the left side of the scroll led by Anubis; and before Osiris, who is seated under an elaborate structure on the far right side of the image.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What detail in this scene shown refers directly to the Egyptian practice of mummification?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/506098f63818381523cfa2979e978ac7.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The white platform represents natron, the natural salts deposited in the Nile, which the ancient Egyptians used in the mummification process.

choices.content: the white platform where the figures stand
the eye in the upper left corner

assessment_items.question_content:
**What material was used to make this Book of the Dead?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This scroll was made from a plant called papyrus, which served as a common writing surface for texts until the MIddle Ages.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What evidence in this image demonstrates that Hunefer has led an ethical life and will be admitted to the afterlife?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Seen on the scales in the image, Hunefer's heart weighs less than a feather, thus proving he has led an ethical life.

choices.content: His heart weighs less than a feather.
Osiris forgives him of his sins.
Thoth finds his name on a list.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was the function of Books of the Dead in the New Kingdom?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Egyptian Books of the Dead were sacred texts that included spells, prayers, incantations and other things the dead needed in the afterlife.

choices.content: to provide guidance and help in the afterlife
to document ethical actions done in one's lifetime
to serve as a memorial for one's family
to document the life of the deceased
